name: Journey Through the Underworld | Egyptian Mythology
summary: Explore the ancient texts, scriptures, and sacred writings.
structure: Guidebook - Divided into twelve hours depicting the sun god's nocturnal journey
influence: Foundation of Egyptian afterlife beliefs; influenced later Jewish and Christian eschatology
icon:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 64 64">
  <circle cx="32" cy="32" r="28.8" fill="#9370DB" opacity="0.2"/>
  <circle cx="32" cy="32" r="19.2" fill="none" stroke="#9370DB" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="5,5"/>

description: Journey Through the Underworld | Egyptian Mythology is an important sacred text in egyptian tradition.
author: Egyptian Priesthood (Anonymous)
alternateNames: Book of What is in the Duat

keyMyths.title: The Weighing of the Heart
keyMyths.description: Anubis weighs the heart of the deceased against the feather of Ma'at (truth). If the heart is heavier than the feather, the soul is devoured by Ammit, the Devourer of Souls, preventing eternal life.

keyMyths.title: Ra's Nightly Journey
keyMyths.description: Each night, Ra, the sun god, travels through the underworld in his solar barque, battling Apophis, the serpent of chaos. His successful navigation ensures the sun rises again in the morning, symbolizing rebirth and order overcoming chaos.

keyMyths.title: Osiris's Resurrection
keyMyths.description: Osiris, murdered and dismembered by his brother Set, is resurrected by Isis through her magical abilities and the help of other deities. This myth establishes Osiris as the ruler of the underworld and a symbol of resurrection and eternal life.

keyMyths.title: The Trials of the Duat
keyMyths.description: The deceased must navigate treacherous landscapes and overcome monstrous guardians within the Duat (underworld). Success in these trials proves their worthiness to enter the afterlife and join Osiris.

keyMyths.title: The Field of Reeds (Aaru)
keyMyths.description: The Field of Reeds is a paradise-like realm within the afterlife, a mirror image of the mortal world but filled with abundance and eternal joy. It is the ultimate destination for those who pass the judgment of Osiris.

sources.name: Book of the Dead
sources.description: A collection of spells, hymns, and prayers intended to guide the deceased through the underworld and ensure their successful passage into the afterlife.
sources.title: Book of the Dead

sources.name: Amduat
sources.description: An important funerary text describing Ra's nightly journey through the twelve hours of the underworld.
sources.title: Amduat

sources.name: Book of Gates
sources.description: A funerary text detailing the gates and challenges encountered in the Duat, emphasizing the dangers and trials the deceased must face.
sources.title: Book of Gates

sources.name: Plutarch, Isis and Osiris
sources.description: A Greek account of the Osiris myth, providing valuable insights into the Egyptian beliefs surrounding death, resurrection, and the afterlife.
sources.title: Plutarch, Isis and Osiris

sources.name: Coffin Texts
sources.description: A collection of spells and funerary texts inscribed on coffins, providing protection and guidance for the deceased in the afterlife.
sources.title: Coffin Texts

canonical: journey through the underworld | egyptian mythology
